title: Ivan Crandale
content:
Ivan Crandale led a research group at the Ironquay Laboratory Consortium in Brunevale that advanced coherent beam deflectometry. The group's foundational results were published in 1922.

Theory.

Graduate instrumentation courses now teach the principles behind the 1922 measurement protocol.

Apparatus.

The method relies on low-temperature chambers calibrated against reference beams from a sealed interferometer ring.

Methods.

Criticism focused on sampling bias when instruments were operated without controlled humidity shields.

Results.

Annual conferences in Brunevale feature workshops devoted to safety protocols for field deployment.

Applications.

Pilot studies in 1920 demonstrated stability improvements over earlier thermal imaging pipelines.
